name: intercom
description: Work with contacts, conversations, and help center articles in Intercom

envVars.name: INTERCOM_CLIENT_ID
envVars.description: Intercom OAuth Client ID (from your app's Basic information page in the Developer Hub)

tools.name: List Contacts
tools.description: List contacts (users and leads) in the Intercom workspace

starting_after.description: Cursor from pages.next.starting_after in a previous response

historicalSummary.omitted: custom attributes, company associations, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Search Contacts
tools.description: Search Intercom contacts with a field/operator/value query

query.description: Search query, e.g. { "field": "email", "operator": "=", "value": "jane@acme.com" } or a nested AND/OR query

tools.name: Get Contact
tools.description: Get a single Intercom contact by ID

tools.name: List Conversations
tools.description: List conversations in the Intercom workspace, newest first

starting_after.description: Cursor from pages.next.starting_after in a previous response

tools.name: Reply To Conversation
tools.description: Reply to an Intercom conversation as an admin (comment or internal note)

message_type.description: Reply type: 'comment' (visible to the user) or 'note' (internal)

admin_id.description: ID of the admin authoring the reply

tools.name: List Articles
tools.description: List help center articles in the Intercom workspace

prompts.title: Recent conversations
prompts.prompt: List the most recent Intercom conversations and summarize what customers are asking about.

prompts.title: Find a contact
prompts.prompt: Search Intercom for a contact by email and show their profile.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Intercom OAuth Setup

steps.title: Create an Intercom app
steps.description: Sign in to Intercom and open the Developer Hub (https://developers.intercom.com/ → Your apps). Create a new app — use a development workspace for testing.

steps.title: Enable OAuth and set the redirect URL
steps.description: In the app's Authentication page, click Edit, enable 'Use OAuth', and add your redirect URL ending in /api/auth/intercom/callback.

steps.title: Select permissions
steps.description: On the same Authentication page, tick the permissions the tools need: read/write contacts, read/list conversations, reply to conversations, and read articles. Permissions are configured here, not via OAuth scopes.

steps.title: Copy credentials and verify
steps.description: Copy the Client ID and Client Secret from Basic information into INTERCOM_CLIENT_ID and INTERCOM_CLIENT_SECRET, connect your workspace, and run the List Contacts tool.

notes: Intercom access tokens are long-lived: they do not expire and no refresh tokens are issued; reauthorize to rotate
Permissions are configured on the app in the Developer Hub; the authorize URL carries no scope parameter
Requests default to the API version pinned on your app; you can override per-request with the Intercom-Version header
